name: Mediterranean Stuffed Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
Prepare sauce: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add onions and green peppers; continue to sauté until softened. Add zucchini, eggplant, and tomatoes; cook until vegetables are tender. Stir in parsley, salt, pepper, thyme, and chicken stock. Reduce heat to low and simmer for approximately 40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens.
Prepare vegetable stuffing: Clean and chop broccoli, cauliflower, and zucchini into bite-sized pieces. Blanch in boiling water for 5 minutes; drain well. Return vegetables to hot skillet with melted butter, salt, and pepper. Set aside.
Make bread crumbs: Pulse bread slices in a food processor until finely crumbly. Set aside.
Prepare meatballs: In a large bowl, mix ground meat, egg, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Form into small balls; place a blanched vegetable in the center of each ball. Dip meatball into egg, then coat with bread crumbs. Heat oil for frying in a deep skillet to 350°F (175°C). Carefully lower meatballs into hot oil; fry until golden brown, about 6-8 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Assemble: Serve meatballs over cooked spaghetti or pasta. Drizzle sauce over the top. Sprinkle with additional parmesan cheese if desired. Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.
